name: PACIFIC GRACE
narrative: VLCC anchored in Gulf of Oman east of Hormuz entrance. P&I war risk exit effective Mar 5 halted transit. Escort queue now 11 vessels deep. Day 9: still at anchor.

name: CASPIAN EXPLORER
narrative: Suezmax in Trump escort convoy queue, position 9 of 14. Estimated transit window Mar 11. Charter rate $95,000/day vs $18,000 pre-crisis. Owner absorbing $1.2M/day holding cost.

name: EASTERN PIONEER
narrative: VLCC laden 2.1M bbl Iraqi Basra crude. Diverted via Cape of Good Hope Day 4. ETA Rotterdam +22 days vs original schedule. Extra voyage cost ~$5.2M. Charterer absorbing.

name: AEGEAN STAR
narrative: Greek-owned Suezmax, Saudi crude to Rotterdam via Cape. First Cape divert for this vessel in 15 years. War risk premium $3.2M added to voyage. Owner in dispute with charterer on cost allocation.

name: GULF SOVEREIGN
narrative: Saudi Aramco VLCC. Completed loading at Ras Tanura Mar 3. Cannot depart — P&I clubs suspended war risk coverage Mar 5. Saudi energy ministry coordinating alternative routing options.

name: BASRA NOBLE
narrative: Iraqi crude Suezmax, departed Khor al-Amaya terminal Day 7. Moving south through Persian Gulf but Hormuz de facto closed. Considering Cape divert from Gulf of Oman. Iraq output already -60% (Bloomberg Mar 8).

name: GULF PRIDE
narrative: VLCC laden with UAE crude. Anchored southern Persian Gulf since Hormuz blockade Day 2. Cargo heating required daily — bunker cost rising. Charter party dispute over demurrage commencing.

name: CAPE BULKER
narrative: Diverted Day 3, carrying Kuwaiti crude to South Korea via Cape. +18 days voyage, $4.8M added cost. Part of growing Cape-route traffic surge — VLCC spot rates now at $85,000/day.

name: YAMUNA EXPRESS
narrative: Indian-chartered VLCC, Iraqi crude to Jamnagar refinery. India largest remaining Gulf crude buyer. Rerouted via Cape; Reliance Industries absorbing $3.9M extra voyage cost.

name: STRAIT GUARDIAN
narrative: Joined Trump naval escort program Mar 7. Convoy position 14 of 18. Estimated transit window Mar 12. Insurance reinstated via specialist Lloyd's war risk syndicate at 0.75% on value.

name: ARABIAN DAWN
narrative: UAE-flagged Suezmax, loaded at Fujairah STS. Heading southeast to join Cape route traffic. Owner elected not to wait for escort — cargo buyer ENOC insisting on delivery.

name: KARACHI WIND
narrative: UAE crude to Karachi refinery via alternative routing. Pakistan state energy securing supply from UAE sovereign sources. Fuel price protest in Karachi on Day 8.

name: HORMUZ TITAN
narrative: In US Navy escort convoy, northbound lane. Transit expected in ~12h. Speed limited to convoy 9.5 knots. Cargo: 2.4M bbl Iraqi crude for Sinopec Ningbo. First convoy transit this vessel.

name: AL SAFLIYA
narrative: QatarEnergy Q-Max. First cargo since force majeure Mar 2. Partial restart authorized Day 9. Cargo: 266,000 cbm LNG. Buyer: JERA Japan. Hormuz passage under US escort scheduled Mar 11.

name: AL DAFNA
narrative: Second QatarEnergy partial restart cargo. Loading completion delayed — Iranian drone strike damaged one berth crane Mar 3. QatarEnergy loading master confirms sailing within 48h.

name: AL GHARRAFA
narrative: Cleared Ras Laffan, navigating south through Persian Gulf toward Hormuz under escort. ETA Hormuz narrows ~18h. Buyer: POSCO Korea. Being shadowed by IRGCN vessel — US destroyer positioned as deterrent.

name: ARCTIC AURORA
narrative: Norwegian Q-Flex LNG awaiting escort. Cargo: 138,000 cbm. European buyer Uniper. War risk premium $2.1M. European LNG inventory at record seasonal lows — buyer under severe pressure.

name: CAPITAL GAS
narrative: Cape-diverted LNG carrier. Original Japan cargo rerouted to Europe to capture TTF premium. Shell Trading spot buyer. ETA Zeebrugge +28 days. Speed maximum to capitalize on price window.

name: EXCELLENCE LNG
narrative: Cape-diverted LNG heading to Rotterdam. Added 21 days voyage time. War risk insurance: $1.8M surcharge absorbed by European importer Equinor. Original destination was Incheon, South Korea.

name: LNG BONNY
narrative: Nigerian LNG diverted via Gulf of Oman to avoid Hormuz. New discharge: Zeebrugge. Original buyer Incheon released cargo to spot market. ETA +32 days vs original schedule.

name: NORTHERN LIGHTS LNG
narrative: Norwegian LNG bypassing Hormuz entirely. Equinor spot buyer at TTF+$8.50/MMBtu. Original destination Incheon renegotiated. One of 14 LNG carriers now on Cape route vs 2 pre-crisis.

name: GRACE BIRKENES
narrative: Departing Gulf of Oman southbound, Cape route confirmed. Was originally heading to Japan. Spot charter to TotalEnergies for French terminal delivery at TTF premium. Speed up to maximize arbitrage.

name: AL YARMOUK
narrative: Kuwaiti VLCC, 2.16M bbl Kuwait Export Crude loaded at Mina al-Ahmadi. Stranded in northern Persian Gulf since Hormuz closure. Kuwait Petroleum Corp in emergency talks with Chinese consignee CNOOC.

name: AL KOUT
narrative: Sister vessel to AL YARMOUK. Kuwait Petroleum Corp VLCC fully laden, anchored in central Persian Gulf. SK Energy buyer pressing for delivery — South Korea holds 97 days strategic reserve. War risk premium unresolved.

name: ORBITER
narrative: VLCC (ex-BASRA, 298,981 DWT). Last port Mina al-Fahl, Oman Mar 7. Anchored off Muscat awaiting owner instructions. Cargo origin undisclosed — vessel under Lloyd's List investigation for possible sanctions-evasion links.

name: PANTANASSA
narrative: Minerva Marine VLCC (317,106 DWT). Fixed at record $436,000/day spot rate — highest VLCC spot charter ever recorded. Cleared Gulf of Oman Day 7, now Cape-diverted. Owner expects $8.7M single-voyage profit at current rates.

name: BAHRI AMAD
narrative: Bahri VLCC (298,705 DWT, built 2018). One of five Bahri VLCCs confirmed stranded inside the Persian Gulf as of Mar 8 (Mitsui OSK: 5 VLCCs; Bahri: 5 VLCCs stranded). Saudi Aramco chartering department coordinating fleet-wide rerouting plan.

name: BAHRI KARAN
narrative: Second Bahri VLCC stranded in Persian Gulf. Saudi Aramco cargo of Arab Light crude. Bahri CEO confirmed five vessels are idle inside the gulf with no near-term transit solution. Bunker consumption cost at $45,000/day.

name: DHT TIGER
narrative: DHT Holdings VLCC (IMO 9733959, 299,629 DWT). Spot market employment. Departed Gulf of Oman Day 5, now deep in Arabian Sea on Cape route. DHT shares up 38% since crisis — DHT has no vessels stranded inside Gulf.

name: FRONT CLOUD
narrative: Frontline VLCC (299,445 DWT, built 2016). Spot market. Frontline stock +31% week-on-week as Cape-route VLCC rates surge. CEO John Fredriksen: 'We have not had a week like this in the 30-year history of the company.'
